stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the highway route) yields a guaranteed return of $192K. Option B (the scenic route) depends on traffic conditions: with probability 31-52% (uncertain), it yields $366K; otherwise it yields $54K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in traffic conditions affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $192K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $150.7K to $216.2K. Point estimate: $185.0K. Option A is preferred at most assumptions (EV_A > EV_B when P(good) < 44.2%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 44.2%, which falls within the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
